positional_bias.note: max_share is exactly 0.40 (not strictly greater than 0.40), so the hard-fail threshold is not triggered; position 2 held by q1 and q5.

length_bias.detail: q5 correct answer (~131 chars) marginally exceeds 1.20× distractor mean (~130.4). One of five MCQs flagged — rate 0.20, well below 0.40 hard-fail threshold.

weakest_questions.distractor: It tells Anthropic's servers to pause billing for ten seconds so the rate-limit quota refills faster.
weakest_questions.note: Implausible (local sleep cannot communicate with remote billing), but still topically grounded — not absurd.

weakest_questions.distractor: It cancels any in-flight requests from the previous attempt and resets the SDK connection pool before the next try.
weakest_questions.note: Conflates time.sleep with network-layer operations; plausible enough to score 2, not 1.

lo_coverage.lo_text: List the four main categories of API failure (network, authentication, rate-limit, server) and describe what causes each one.
Describe the Anthropic SDK exception hierarchy — how AnthropicError, APIError, and the specific error classes relate to one another.
Wrap a client.messages.create() call in a try/except block that catches at least two specific Anthropic exception classes.

Distinguish the correct response for each exception type: fix credentials for AuthenticationError, wait and retry for RateLimitError, inspect the request for BadRequestError, check connectivity for APIConnectionError.
Implement a basic retry loop for RateLimitError using time.sleep().
Explain why catching the most-specific exception class first leads to clearer, safer error-handling code.

scope_and_beginner_fairness.notes: All stems and distractors are grounded in corpus-taught concepts only. No acronyms introduced without prior definition. q6 short-answer uses multiple = lines (valid GIFT syntax for acceptable-answer variants).

verdict: Pass — positional bias at exactly 0.40 (not above threshold), length bias 0.20, zero absurd distractors, all six LOs covered, no orphan questions, no scope violations.
